p.strong: Pool Allocator (O(1)) vs malloc/free (O(n)) Performance Measurement

div.p: c #define NUM_ITERATIONS 1000000 #define BLOCK_SIZE 64 #define POOL_CAPACITY 10000 #define CLOCK_SOURCE CLOCK_MONOTONIC

Technical Conclusion: The pool allocator achieves deterministic O(1) allocation/deallocation by leveraging fixed-size blocks. This eliminates search overhead and fragmentation management found in glibc malloc.

g.title: Traverse bin lists to find free chunk
